text: Ok, this reactor is R4 reactor. This is the assembly process of this reactor. Step 1.
This is the cathode flow plate. It has a splitting flow tube. Step 2. This is the cathode electrode.
The cathode is prepared by spray coating cobalt oxide catalysis onto the H2C3 carbon paper.
The catalytic loading is 3 mg per square. Step 3. This is the cathode gasket. The gasket
thickness is 0.3 mm and the ion window size is 1 by 1 cm. Step 4. We will place the alkaline
ion exchange membrane. The membrane is Stenol X3750.
This is the AM, the membrane. Step 5. This is the middle chamber. The chamber thickness
is 10 mm and the window size is 1 by 1 cm. The chamber is used to hold the liquid electrolyte.
The liquid electrolyte is 1 molar QOH and it will be continuously circulated through
the middle chamber during operation. This is the inlet and this is the outlet. Step
6. We add the anode gasket. The anode gasket is also 0.3 mm. Step 7. We also place the
This membrane is also Stenol X3750.
Purchased from Dioxide Materials. Step 8. This is the anode electrode. The anode is
also prepared by spray coating but using Pt-Rosilium carbon catalysis onto H23C3 carbon
paper. The catalytic loading is 2 mg per cm2. Step 9. This is the anode flow plate. It also
has a flow field. Finally, after aligning all components, we will assemble this reactor.
We assemble on the tightened reactor using a torque of 0.3 Nm. The reactor is tightened
using 4 bolts in a diagonal sequence to ensure uniform compression. The key change in this
reactor is that the middle solid electrolyte layer is replaced by a circulating 1 molecule
H liquid electrolyte. The purpose is to improve ionic activity in the middle chamber and reduce
the self-resistance during operation.
